title: Moving to Gainesville, FL? Rekey Your Locks Before You Unpack
excerpt: Moving to Gainesville, FL? Rekeying your locks is the smartest first step before settling in. Be Secure Locksmith explains why it matters and how the process works.
author: Netta Kaiden
metaTitle: Moving to Gainesville? Rekey Your Locks Before You Unpack | Be Secure Locksmith
metaDescription: Moving to Gainesville, FL? Rekeying your locks is the smartest first step before settling in. Be Secure Locksmith explains why it matters and how the process works.
body: Your first night in a new home should feel secure, not uncertain. When you move into a property in Gainesville, FL, you have no way of knowing how many copies of your keys are still out there. Previous owners, past tenants, contractors, and real estate agents may have held copies for months or years. Taking control of who can access your home starts with a simple, affordable rekey appointment on move-in day.

Be Secure Locksmith provides mobile lock rekeying services throughout Gainesville and the surrounding areas, so you don’t have to add another errand to an already packed moving day. Our techs come to you, work through each lock on-site, and leave you with a fresh set of keys before you even hang your first picture.
What Does It Mean to Rekey Locks After Moving in Gainesville, FL?

Rekeying is the process of adjusting the internal pins inside your lock cylinder so that it only responds to a new key. The hardware on your door stays exactly the same. A trained locksmith disassembles the cylinder, replaces the driver and key pins to match a new key cut, reassembles the lock, and tests it before moving on to the next door.

Because no new hardware is needed, lock rekeying in Gainesville typically costs less than a full lock replacement. For most homeowners moving into a property with functioning locks in good condition, rekeying is all that’s needed to restore full control over who holds a working key.
If your new home has locks that are worn, damaged, or outdated, our techs can assess them during the same visit and advise whether a full lock installation makes more sense for any entry points.
Why Can’t You Just Change Your Locks Instead?

Full lock replacement is a solid option when locks are old, damaged, or you want a higher-security upgrade. But if the locks on your new Gainesville home are in good working condition, rekeying gets you the same outcome at a lower cost. Old keys stop working, and only the new keys you hold will open the door.

Some homeowners moving into newer construction find that builder-grade locks are still relatively new but have been passed through multiple hands during the sale process. Rekeying neutralizes every copied key without touching the hardware at all.

If you do want to upgrade, smart lock installation is worth considering at the time of your move. A keypad or app-controlled lock removes the key-sharing problem entirely and gives you access logs and remote control from day one.
Which Doors Should You Rekey When You Move?

A thorough rekey covers every exterior entry point on the home. Our techs will walk through the property with you and note every lock that needs attention. Here is what a standard move-in rekey includes:

• Front door deadbolt and knob lock
  • Back door and any side entry doors
  • Door leading from an attached garage into the living space
  • Detached garage doors with keyed locks

Interior locks are generally not a priority unless the home has a private room, a home office, or a rental unit component. If you want all exterior doors keyed to a single key for convenience, that is a standard part of the service.
Is Rekeying Enough, or Do You Also Need to Upgrade Your Locks?

For most residential moves, rekeying is enough. But move-in day is a good time to look at the overall condition and quality of the locks already installed. Our residential locksmith services in Gainesville cover everything from rekeying to full hardware upgrades in a single visit.

It is also worth checking your door frame and strike plate during this process. A high-quality lock on a weak frame does not provide the protection you would expect. Common issues our techs flag during move-in appointments include:

• Strike plates held in by short screws that pull out easily
  • Worn or cracked door jambs that reduce resistance to forced entry
  • Low-grade builder hardware with minimal security ratings
  • Locks that show signs of tampering or damage

If you are moving into a property you plan to manage as a rental, our property management locksmith services can help you set up a rekeying schedule and master key system that keeps things organized across multiple units.
How Quickly Can You Schedule a Rekey Appointment in Gainesville?

We serve Gainesville and the surrounding North Central Florida region with mobile service, meaning there is no shop to visit and no waiting around. You set a time, and a tech comes to the property. For a move-in rekey covering standard exterior doors, most appointments take well under two hours from start to finish.

Scheduling early in the moving process is the right call. There is no reason to spend even one night unsure of who else has a key. A rekey appointment can often be booked for the same day you pick up the keys from your agent or closing attorney.
Start Fresh in Your New Gainesville Home

Rekeying locks after moving to Gainesville, FL takes the uncertainty out of your first weeks in a new home. You get full control over who holds a working key, without replacing hardware that is already doing its job. It is a fast, mobile, and cost-effective service that belongs at the top of your move-in checklist.

Be Secure Locksmith is locally owned and serves Gainesville, Alachua, Newberry, High Springs, and the broader North Central Florida region. Our techs come equipped to handle every exterior lock on your property during a single visit, and we test every lock before we leave. Contact us to request mobile lock rekeying service at your new Gainesville address.
